European Commission (DG EMPL) has published the 2025 edition of the Annual Report on Intra-EU Labour Mobility , presenting updated insights on labour mobility across EU and EFTA countries based on 2023–2024 data. The report examines trends in the mobile working-age population, including flows, characteristics, labour market

g-age population, including flows, characteristics, labour market integration, and long-term mobility. Of particular importance are the highlighted features of cross-border workers. orms Linguistic leave is a special form of leave granted to employees of all nationalities who wish to learn Luxembourgish or to improve their knowledge of the language. Linguistic leave forms part of occupational training in Luxembourg in addition to individual training leave . Who is concerned The following people are eligib

beral profession in Luxembourg for at least 6 months already . There are no age or residence requirements for applicants for linguistic leave. The Luxemburgish language courses eligible for linguistic leave are those provided in Luxembourg or abroad by: public or private teaching establishments recognised by the public authori

fession in Luxembourg for at least 6 months already . There are no age or residence requirements for applicants for linguistic leave. The Luxemburgish language courses eligible for linguistic leave are those provided in Luxembourg or abroad by: public or private teaching establishments recognised by the public authorities and

private associations specifically approved by the Minister responsible for vocational training; ministries, administrations and public establishments.
